task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the title slide with the correct title and subtitle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide containing the title 'Hurricane Ian Emergency Briefing' and the subtitle 'Coastal County EMA'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Current Storm Status' slide contains the correct wind and movement data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide titled 'Current Storm Status' that includes the text '155' (for mph/winds) and 'NNE' (for movement).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Forecast Track' slide contains the track cone image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide titled 'Forecast Track' that displays an inserted image of a hurricane track cone.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Storm Surge Threat' slide contains the surge image and expected peak surge text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide titled 'Storm Surge Threat' that displays an inserted image of a storm surge map and includes text mentioning '12-18' (feet).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Evacuation Orders' slide lists the correct mandatory evacuation zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide titled 'Evacuation Orders' that explicitly lists 'Zone A' and 'Zone B'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Shelter Operations' slide lists the correct shelters and includes the required speaker note.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a slide titled 'Shelter Operations' mentioning 'High School' and 'Civic Center'. Additionally, look at the speaker notes pane at the bottom of the screen to verify it contains text mentioning '45 mph'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presentation is saved with the correct filename and has exactly 6 slides.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the application title bar or save dialog for the filename 'ian_briefing.pptx' and check the slide thumbnail pane on the left to confirm there are exactly 6 slides.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a graphical presentation application (like OnlyOffice, LibreOffice Impress, or PowerPoint) to create the slides.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the UI of a presentation software being actively used, rather than just a terminal window running a Python script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify that the images were actually inserted and rendered on the slides.
integrity.visual_evidence: The forecast track and storm surge images should be visible as graphics on their respective slides, not just as file paths or text placeholders.

integrity.description: Verify the speaker note was placed in the actual speaker notes section.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text about '45 mph' should be located in the dedicated speaker notes pane (usually at the bottom of the presentation UI), not typed as regular text on the main slide canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The presentation must be saved as 'ian_briefing.pptx' and contain exactly 6 slides. Slide 1 must include 'Hurricane Ian' and 'Coastal County'. Slide 2 must include '155' and 'NNE'. Slide 4 must include '12-18'. Slide 5 must include 'Zone A' and 'Zone B'. Slide 6 must include 'High School' and 'Civic Center', and its speaker notes must mention '45 mph'.
